_schema: Community programs directory. Fields: id, name, expanded_name (acronyms spelled out on first contact — newcomer requirement), summary (from live-site copy), details[] (bullet specifics), audience[] (controlled vocab, same as events), cta {label, href}, external_url (live babson.edu page). filter_tags: the simple 4-term audience filter (Students/Faculty/Entrepreneurs/Business Owners).

programs.name: Big Ideas — the Explorers
programs.summary: The Generator is co-led by Babson's best and brightest students — visionaries, entrepreneurs, technologists, strategists, coders and prototypers. We call them Explorers.
details: Explorers lead AI programming in partnership with business owners, entrepreneurs, corporate partners, VCs, technologists, and futurists
Explorers organize the large-scale Entrepreneurship & AI Buildathons with Microsoft Research, engaging Babson, Stanford, Olin, and Wellesley students
Explorer-led initiatives focus on themes from the eight Specialty Labs
Buildathon winners Terraflex — an AI-powered adaptive prosthetic built by Olin and Stanford students — took the $2,500 first prize and the $60,000 Nature-Inspired Design Prize

programs.name: AI Credits
programs.expanded_name: Free AI compute for entrepreneurial projects
programs.summary: Two pathways to secure free credits for AI projects — real money for real builds.
details: $1,000 in AI compute from Babson: one-time student grants with a fast turnaround process
$1,000–$25,000 in AI credits via the Microsoft for Startups Founders Hub partnership
Promising ventures may receive $5,000 in AI compute without meeting the incorporation requirement

programs.name: B.R.A.I.N.
programs.summary: Unites industry and higher education to explore the entrepreneurial, social and ethical dimensions of AI. Periodic meetups throughout New England connect leaders, business owners, entrepreneurs, technologists, and AI researchers.
details: Driving economic growth through AI-powered entrepreneurship
Supporting small business owners to leverage AI for business growth
Understanding the ethical dimensions of AI
A big tent: Babson alumni particularly welcome, but anyone can attend

programs.name: AI Innovators Bootcamp
programs.expanded_name: Training 1,000 small business owners in AI
programs.summary: A 1-day deep dive into AI taught by Babson's award-winning faculty, delivered in partnership with Babson's top students. 93% of small business owners say they are not prepared for the AI-age; this program is the answer.
details: Core concepts in AI, no prerequisites — built for owners starting from zero
Hands-on-keys activities supported by Babson faculty and students
AI complexities, ethics and risks included, not glossed over
Ends with an AI-networking cocktail reception and a cohort community of 30+ owners

programs.name: Intern Opportunities
programs.expanded_name: Paid student roles inside the Specialty Labs
programs.summary: Labs post research, design, and communications internships — from multilingual AI models to design futuring to Republic 2.0, the Ethics lab's city of the future.
details: AI Entrepreneurship & Business Innovation: Multilingual AI Models Intern, Social Media Intern
Work Futures: Research, Design, and Communications interns
AI Ethics & Society: Future Cities (Republic 2.0) Research and Design Intern
AI & ML Empowerment: positions currently filled — check back in September

programs.name: CollaboratorAI
programs.summary: A tool that helps Babson faculty identify common research interests and explore collaboration opportunities using AI. It may not always be completely accurate or up-to-date — verify important information independently.

programs.name: Leadership
programs.expanded_name: The faculty team behind The Generator
programs.summary: The Generator is led by an interdisciplinary Babson faculty team which oversees its research, experimentation, programming, and partnerships — the same faculty who lead the eight Specialty Labs.
